items.title: Binary confusion matrix layout
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  actual0["actual class 0 (negative)"]
  actual1["actual class 1 (positive)"]
  pred0["predicted 0"]
  pred1["predicted 1"]
  tnCell["TN: actual 0, pred 0"]
  fpCell["FP: actual 0, pred 1"]
  fnCell["FN: actual 1, pred 0"]
  tpCell["TP: actual 1, pred 1"]
  actual0 --> pred0 --> tnCell
  actual0 --> pred1 --> fpCell
  actual1 --> pred0 --> fnCell
  actual1 --> pred1 --> tpCell
items.caption: Four disjoint outcomes partition the evaluation set; every example lands in exactly one cell.

items.title: From scores to hard labels to counts
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  scoresNode["model scores s"]
  threshNode["threshold t: pred 1 if s >= t"]
  compareNode["compare to y_true"]
  matrixNode["tally TP, FP, TN, FN"]
  scoresNode --> threshNode
  threshNode --> compareNode
  compareNode --> matrixNode
items.caption: Logistic regression and other classifiers produce scores; one chosen threshold yields one confusion matrix.

items.title: Row and column margins
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  row0["actual 0 row: TN + FP = all negatives"]
  row1["actual 1 row: FN + TP = all positives"]
  col0["pred 0 col: TN + FN"]
  col1["pred 1 col: FP + TP"]
  tprNode["TPR = TP / (TP + FN) uses row 1"]
  fprNode["FPR = FP / (FP + TN) uses row 0"]
  precNode["Precision = TP / (TP + FP) uses col 1"]
  row1 --> tprNode
  row0 --> fprNode
  col1 --> precNode
items.caption: TPR and FPR read from actual rows; precision reads from the predicted-positive column.
